scope: Drag-Scale Adaptive EKF (DSA-EKF): rule, structural process model, validation tuning grid, and decision criterion predeclared before evaluation on the higher-fidelity force-model-mismatch slice (truth-side J2..J6 zonal geopotential, luni-solar third-body, exponential drag with diurnal-bulge density modulation; estimator keeps the compact two-body+J2+exponential-drag deterministic flow with the drag acceleration scaled by an online-estimated multiplicative factor beta).
estimator: DSA-EKF -- seven-state Extended Kalman Filter on the augmented state [r, v, beta], where beta is a multiplicative drag-scale parameter modelled as a first-order Gauss-Markov process about its nominal value of one, with steady-state standard deviation drag_scale_sigma_ss and decorrelation time drag_scale_tau_s. The deterministic flow uses the same compact two-body+J2+drag acceleration as every classical baseline in this study, with the drag acceleration scaled by beta; the estimator absorbs drag/density/ballistic-coefficient bias through this structural channel rather than through innovation-driven measurement-noise reweighting or a generic Cartesian empirical-acceleration channel.
rationale: Classical statistical OD (Tapley, Schutz, and Born 2004, Section 10.5; Vallado 2013, Section 9.6.1) prescribes ballistic-coefficient or drag-scale estimation as the operational response to drag/density mismatch in LEO. The loop 44 generic Cartesian empirical-acceleration channel (DMC-EKF) was structurally inert under sparse visibility because the empirical-acceleration channel was essentially observable only through cross-covariance accumulated between visible updates. The DSA-EKF channel scales the drag acceleration directly so the channel is observable wherever drag is, which is the entire orbit between the deepest atmospheric perigees down to the LEO drag-noise floor.

rule.augmented_state: [r_x, r_y, r_z, v_x, v_y, v_z, beta]
rule.process_model: dr/dt = v, dv/dt = a_grav(r) + a_J2(r) + beta * a_drag(r, v), dbeta/dt = -(beta - 1) / drag_scale_tau_s
rule.decision_predicate: DSA-EKF is a positive contribution if and only if (i) its mean observed-step position RMSE is strictly the lowest among {EKF, UKF, AUKF, PUKF, DMC-EKF, DSA-EKF} on the higher-fidelity force-mismatch slice, AND (ii) the paired-bootstrap 95% CI for the DSA-EKF-minus-best-non-DSA gap is strictly negative (DSA-EKF strictly better), AND (iii) the absolute gap exceeds the predeclared 3% practical-significance floor of the best-non-DSA mean.
rule.honest_negative: If the predeclared rule does not yield a positive in the sense above, it is reported as a bounded structural-channel negative (no rule retuning is allowed after the held-out test evaluation).

validation_tuning.purpose: Select the drag-scale Gauss-Markov hyperparameters before the held-out test. The validation population is generated on a validation seed disjoint from every test or training seed used in the manuscript and disjoint from the model-selection splits.
validation_tuning.selection_rule: Choose the grid point with the lowest mean observed-step position RMSE on the validation population. The selected hyperparameters are frozen before the held-out test; no test-set information enters the selection.

thresholds_justification: The drag-scale process noise sigma=0.5 corresponds to a roughly 50% standard deviation around the nominal ballistic-coefficient/drag-density product, comfortably covering the truth-side density mismatch (truth uses 7e-11 vs estimator 4e-11 reference density, i.e. ~1.75x bias) plus the diurnal-bulge modulation. The Gauss-Markov decorrelation time 600 s is roughly one-tenth of an orbital period (a small fraction of the orbit, the operational LEO ballistic-coefficient estimation default). The Kalman gating threshold and position/velocity process-noise standard deviations match the EKF baseline exactly so the only structural change is the multiplicative drag channel.

evaluation_protocol.data: Higher-fidelity force-model mismatch slice: truth uses two-body + J2..J6 zonal geopotential + luni-solar third-body acceleration + exponential drag with a one-sided diurnal-bulge density modulation (alpha=0.30); estimators keep the compact two-body+J2+exponential-drag deterministic flow.
evaluation_protocol.primary_endpoint: Observed-step position RMSE on a held-out test seed disjoint from the validation tuning seed.
